constraints: Every order MUST be inserted; fallback to new Assign if no existing Assign works.
content:
API: insertships_skeleton
Rules:
- Save Assign state before trial AddShip.
- If AddShip succeeds: GenRoute, record cost_delta, then RemoveShip+GenRoute to undo.
- Commit: re-apply best (Assign, position) once. GenRoute after final insert.
- Every order needs a fallback insertion path.
- Call RenewnTotalCost exactly once before return.
summary: Safe Go API call sequence: save state, trial insert, record delta, rollback, commit best, RenewnTotalCost.
title: InsertShips Go API skeleton

constraints: Never lose or duplicate orders during vehicle-to-vehicle moves.
content:
API: optimization_skeleton
Rules:
- Use dispatch.Assigns[].RemoveShip/AddShip/GenRoute to move orders between vehicles.
- Use dispatch.RenewnTotalCost() before return.
- Temperature parameter controls acceptance of worse moves (SA).
- Return the modified dispatch. Never create new Assign objects from scratch.
summary: SA-style route improvement: move orders between vehicles, preserve dispatch integrity.
title: Optimization Go API skeleton

constraints: Total selected weight must NOT exceed capacity.
content:
API: knapsack_selectitems_skeleton
Rules:
- func SelectItems(items []Item, capacity int) []bool
- Return a boolean slice of length len(items).
- selected weight := sum(items[i].Weight for i where result[i] is true).
- selected must be <= capacity.
- Maximize total value := sum(items[i].Value for i where result[i] is true).
summary: 0/1 knapsack: return boolean array, respect capacity, maximize value.
title: Knapsack SelectItems Go API skeleton

constraints: Preserve each original order volume exactly.
Every suborder volume must be positive and <= chosen vehicle capacity.
content:
API: mixer_split_skeleton
Rules:
- func SplitOrders(orders []Order, vehicles []Vehicle, workHours float64) []SubOrder
- Return []SubOrder.
- Preserve each original order volume exactly.
- Every suborder volume must be <= chosen vehicle capacity.
- Use fallback splitting by largest available vehicle.
- Never invent unknown order IDs.
summary: Concrete mixer order splitting: preserve volume, obey vehicle capacity, return valid suborders.
title: Mixer SplitOrders Go API skeleton

constraints: The evaluator opens a new bin when no existing bin is feasible.
content:
API: obp_scorebin_skeleton
Rules:
- func ScoreBin(item int, remaining []int, capacity int) []float64
- remaining contains only feasible bins.
- Return len(remaining) finite scores; highest score wins.
- Do not read files, env, network, or use randomness.
- Prefer fewer used bins and low gap to lower bound.
summary: Online bin packing: score feasible bins, return finite scores, minimize used bins.
title: Online Bin Packing ScoreBin API skeleton

constraints: Return exactly one int from unvisited_nodes.
Never return a visited node, destination_node, or a new array.
content:
API: tsp_select_next_node
Rules:
- def select_next_node(current_node, destination_node, unvisited_nodes, distance_matrix) -> int
- Return one int contained in unvisited_nodes.
- Use distance_matrix[current_node][unvisited_nodes] for distances.
- Do not mutate unvisited_nodes or distance_matrix.
- Keep computation bounded and deterministic.
summary: TSP construct: return one unvisited node id from select_next_node.
title: Official TSP Construct select_next_node API skeleton

constraints: Return one int from unvisited_nodes, or depot only for voluntary return.
Respect rest_capacity; unvisited_nodes is already capacity-feasible.
content:
API: cvrp_select_next_node
Rules:
- def select_next_node(current_node, depot, unvisited_nodes, rest_capacity, demands, distance_matrix) -> int
- Return one int from unvisited_nodes, or depot to close route.
- Use demands and rest_capacity for capacity-aware choices.
- Do not mutate arrays.
- Keep deterministic and bounded.
summary: CVRP construct: return one feasible customer, or depot only to end a route.
title: Official CVRP Construct select_next_node API skeleton
